title: Death rate from neonatal preterm birth complications
subtitle: The estimated death rate from [neonatal](#dod:neonatal) [preterm birth complications](#dod:preterm-birth-complications) in children under five years old, per 100,000.
caption:
Death rate from neonatal preterm birth complications

The estimated death rate from neonatal preterm birth complications in children under five years old, per 100,000.

This choropleth map displays the estimated death rate from neonatal preterm birth complications in children under five years old, per 100,000, for the year 2023. The visualization uses a continuous color scale ranging from pale yellow to dark blue to represent values across a spectrum from 0 to 1,000 deaths per 100,000. The highest mortality rates, indicated by the darkest blue shades corresponding to the upper end of the scale, are concentrated across sub-Saharan Africa. Countries such as Nigeria, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, and Somalia fall within these highest bands, showing rates that extend well into the hundreds per 100,000. Moving to the middle of the distribution, nations in South Asia, including India and Pakistan, along with parts of the Middle East and Central America, appear in medium teal shades, placing them in the range between 50 and 200 deaths per 100,000. In contrast, the lowest rates are visible across North America, Western Europe, and Australia, where the map shows pale yellow and light green coloring. These regions sit in the lowest bands of the legend, with values generally below 10 or 20 deaths per 100,000. Large portions of Russia and China also display light green shading, indicating rates in the lower-middle range relative to the global maximums. The geographic distribution shows a clear divide, with the burden of mortality from these complications falling heavily on the African continent while remaining low in high-income regions. The data shows the highest values across western and central Africa, with the lowest values across North America and Western Europe.
